mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on equity cash flows and increases borrowing costs for corporations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market reprices equity valuations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-2005.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 0.2%, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth above 0.5% month-over-month indicates robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings growth. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise and equity risk premium compresses.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth does not precede positive SPX returns in at least 52% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.3% month-over-month while remaining positive, signaling supply-side capacity constraints and potential inflation acceleration. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as real discount rates rise and inflation expectations increase.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 48% of instances when INDPRO growth is between 0% and 0.3%, or Spearman rank correlation < 0.01 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. This joint signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate and equity risk premium compresses.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2014.0 thousands, RSAFS at 353866.0 millions as of 2005-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate jointly, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 4.5%, indicating monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real rates compress and inflation expectations rise, eventually forcing policy tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 197.8 index, FEDFUNDS at 4.0% as of 2005-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when inflation exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 4.5%, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.2% while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline and equity risk premium widens.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0%, PAYEMS at 134289.0 thousands as of 2005-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 5.2% and PAYEMS declines, or joint signal has lower predictive power than either signal alone (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1800 thousand units, indicating demand-side weakness in both aggregate and residential sectors. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases and equity valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1800 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11206.1 billions, HOUST at 2014.0 thousands as of 2005-10-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances when both GDPC1 growth < 2.0% and HOUST < 1800, or interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.02).
